description: Schema for Logging Service values configuration

annotations.description: Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata. They are not queryable and should be preserved when modifying objects. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/annotations
Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata.

dockerImage.description: A docker image to use for Cloud Events Reader deployment.

install.description: Allows to enable/disable deploy of Cloud Events Reader.

labels.description: Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects.

nodeSelectorKey.description: Defines which Nodes the Pods are scheduled on. Used with nodeSelectorValue.

nodeSelectorValue.description: Defines which Nodes the Pods are scheduled on. Used with nodeSelectorKey.

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName assigned to the Pods to prevent them from evicting.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cloudURL.description: Cloud address. Openshift cloud may require to set the current host address.

createClusterAdminEntities.description: Specifies whether a cluster-admin entities should be created.

additionalVolumeMounts.description: additionalVolumeMounts allows configuration of additional VolumeMounts on the output DaemonSet definition.

additionalVolumes.description: additionalVolumes allows configuration of additional volumes on the output DaemonSet definition.

affinity.description: If specified, the pod's scheduling constraints

dockerImage.description: A docker image to use for FluentBit aggregator configmap reload sidecar.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

memory.description: Initial amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

customLuaScriptConf.description: FluentBit custom lua script configuration. Note: it's very important to keep the indents.

extraFields.description: Adds additional custom fields/labels to every log message by using filter based on record_modifier plugin.

additionalVolumeMounts.description: additionalVolumeMounts allows configuration of additional VolumeMounts on the output DaemonSet definition. additionalVolumeMounts specified will be appended to other VolumeMounts in the prometheus container, that are generated as a result of StorageSpec objects. additionalVolumes.description: additionalVolumes allows configuration of additional volumes on the output DaemonSet definition. additionalVolumes specified will be appended to other volumes that are generated as a result of StorageSpec objects. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#volume-v1-core

billCycleConf.description: Flag to enable FluentD filter for parsing bill-cycle-logs

cloudEventsReaderFormat.description: Allow to add filter to parse json of Kubernetes events logs from Cloud Events Reader

compress.description: If "gzip" is set, FluentD compresses data records before writing to buffer chunks

dockerImage.description: A docker image to use for configmap reload container in fluentd pod.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

containerLogging.description: Enable input for container logs from /var/logs/containers for Docker or /var/log/pods for other engines.

dockerImage.description: A docker image to use for fluent daemon set.

excludePath.description: Path to exclude logs, it can have multiple values as an array.

extraFields.description: Adds additional custom fields/labels to every log message by using filter based on record_transformer plugin. This parameter will override existing fields if their keys match those specified in extraFields. This filter works after all other filters except the custom filter.

fileStorage.description: Flag for using file storage instead of memory

install.description: Allow disabling create FluentD during deploy

nodeSelectorKey.description: Defines which nodes the pods are scheduled on. Used with nodeSelectorValue

nodeSelectorValue.description: Defines which nodes the pods are scheduled on. Used with nodeSelectorKey

routing.description: Propagates additional information to HTTP outgoing traffic so it will be properly routed further.

enabled.description: Enable HTTP routing.

logCategoryHeader.description: Header name which carries log category and to be used for HTTP routing.

auth.description: Authentication for HTTP output. Secret name and key must be provided for each credential.

password.description: Secret reference for basic auth password.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the password (e.g. 'password').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the password (e.g. 'http-secret').

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the token (e.g. 'token').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the token (e.g. 'http-secret').

user.description: Secret reference for basic auth username.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the username (e.g. 'user').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the username (e.g. 'http-secret').

compress.description: Payload compression mechanism for HTTP output. Allowed values: text, gzip.

enabled.description: Flag for enabling HTTP output.

extraParams.description: Additional configuration parameters for HTTP output (buffer and other Fluentd HTTP output plugin options).

headers.description: Additional headers for HTTP requests as a JSON object string (e.g. '{"VL-Msg-Field": "log", "VL-Time-Field": "time", "VL-Stream-Fields": "stream"}').

host.description: HTTP host URL for ingesting logs.

path.description: HTTP path for url to ingest logs.

ca.description: Configuration for a custom CA store.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the CA certificate (e.g. 'ca.crt').

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the CA certificate (e.g. 'secret-ca').

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the client certificate (e.g. 'certificate.crt').

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the client certificate (e.g. 'secret-cert').

ciphers.description: Allowed TLS ciphers. If empty, uses library defaults.

enabled.description: Enables TLS for HTTP output.

key.description: Configuration for a key for the client certificate.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the key for the client certificate (e.g. 'privateKey.key').

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the key for the client certificate (e.g. 'secret-key').

verifyMode.description: Certificate verification mode. Allowed values: none, peer.

version.description: The default version of TLS. Available values: TLSv1_3(since 1.19.0)/TLSv1_2/TLSv1_1

password.description: Secret reference for Loki basic auth password.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the password (e.g. 'password').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the password (e.g. 'loki-secret').

token.description: Secret reference for Loki bearer token.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the token (e.g. 'token').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the token (e.g. 'loki-secret').

user.description: Secret reference for Loki basic auth username.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the username (e.g. 'user').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the username (e.g. 'loki-secret').

enabled.description: Flag for enabling Loki output.

extraParams.description: Additional configuration parameters for Loki output.

labelsMapping.description: Labels mappings that defines how to extract labels from each log record
examples:
|-
        stream $.stream
        container $.container
        pod $.pod
        namespace $.namespace
        level $.level
        hostname $.hostname
        nodename $.kubernetes_host
        request_id $.request_id
        tenant_id $.tenant_id
        addressTo $.addressTo
        originating_bi_id $.originating_bi_id
        spanId $.spanId

tls.description: FluentD configuration for TLS enabling for Graylog output. Works only for TCP Graylog protocol.

allCiphers.description: Allows any ciphers to be used, may be insecure.

ca.description: Configuration for a custom CA store. If present, both secretName and secretKey are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the CA certificate (e.g. 'ca.crt').

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the CA certificate (e.g. 'secret-ca').

cert.description: Configuration for a client certificate. If present, both secretName and secretKey are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the client certificate (e.g. 'certificate.crt').

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the client certificate (e.g. 'secret-cert').

enabled.description: Allows enabling TLS for out-of-box Graylog GELF output managed by the operator.

generateCerts.description: Allows automatic generation of certificates for the GELF output to Graylog using cert-manager.

duration.description: Specifies the validity period of the requested certificate.

enabled.description: Enables / disables automatic generation of certificates.

renewBefore.description: Defines how long before the certificate’s expiry cert-manager should attempt to renew it.

secretName.description: A secret name which will store certificates

key.description: Configuration for a key for the client certificate. If present, both secretName and secretKey are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the key for the client certificate (e.g. 'privateKey.key').

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the key for the client certificate (e.g. 'secret-key').

noDefaultCA.description: Prevents OpenSSL from using the systems CA store.

rescueSslErrors.description: Similar to rescue_network_errors in notifier.rb, allows SSL exceptions to be raised.

tolerations.description: List of tolerations applied to FluentD Pods.

totalLimitSize.description: The size limitation of output buffer

watchKubernetesMetadata.description: Set up a watch on pods on the API server for updates to metadata.

affinity.description: If specified, the pod's scheduling constraints

authType.description: Defines which type of authentication protocol will be chosen (LDAP or OAuth 2.0).

ldap.description: Configuration for LDAP or AD connection

bindPassword.description: LDAP password for the bind DN.

bindPasswordSecret.description: Allows to define a secret for bind password. Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.24/#secretkeyselector-v1-core

key.description: A key in the secret for bind password.

name.description: A name of secret for bind password.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with CA certificate.

cert.description: Configuration for a client certificate. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with client certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with client certificate.

key.description: Configuration for a key for the certificate file. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with key for the client certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with key for the client certificate.

overSsl.description: Establish an LDAP session over SSL.

skipVerify.description: Allow skipping verification of the LDAP server's certificate.

logLevel.description: Logging level. Allowed values: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL

authorizationPath.description: This path will be used to build URL for redirection to OAuth2 authorization server login page.

ca.description: Configuration for a Secret contains CA certificate for OAuth2 authorization server. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with CA certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with CA certificate.

cert.description: Configuration for a client certificate. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with client certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with client certificate.

clientCredentialsSecret.description: Allows to define a Kubernetes Secret for OAuth client secret.

key.description: A key in the secret with OAuth client secret.

name.description: A name of secret with OAuth client secret.

clientID.description: OAuth2 Client ID for the proxy.

clientSecret.description: OAuth2 Client Secret for the proxy. Will be stored in the secret with .oauth.clientCredentialsSecret.name at key specified in the .oauth.clientCredentialsSecret.key.

key.description: Configuration for a key for the certificate file. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with key for the client certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with key for the client certificate.

redirectUri.description: URI to redirect after successful logging in on the OAuth2 authorization server side. Uses "graylog.host" (the same host as in the Graylog Ingress) with /code path by default. Please make sure that your OAuth server allows using this URI as a redirection URI.

rolesJsonpath.description: JSONPath (by jsonpath-ng) for taking information about entities (roles, groups, etc.) for Graylog roles and streams mapping from the JSON returned from OAuth2 server by using userinfo path. Configured for Keycloak server by default.

scopes.description: OAuth2 scopes for the proxy separated by spaces. Configured for Keycloak server by default.

skipVerify.description: Allow skipping verification of the OAuth2 authorization server's certificate.

tokenPath.description: This path will be used to build URL for getting access token from OAuth2 authorization server.

userJsonpath.description: JSONPath (by jsonpath-ng) for taking username from the JSON returned from OAuth2 server by using userinfo path. Configured for Keycloak server by default.

userinfoPath.description: This path will be used to build URL for getting information about current user from OAuth2 authorization server to get username and entities (roles, groups, etc.) for Graylog roles and streams mapping.

preCreatedUsers.description: Comma separated pre-created users in Graylog for which you do not need to rotate passwords.

requestsTimeout.description: A global timeout parameter affects requests to LDAP server, OAuth server and Graylog server.

resources.description: The resources describe to compute resource requests and limits for graylog-auth-proxy container.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

roleMapping.description: Filter for mapping Graylog roles between LDAP or OAuth and Graylog users.

rotationPassInterval.description: Interval in days between password rotation for non-pre-created users.

streamMapping.description: Filter for sharing Graylog streams between LDAP or OAuth and Graylog users.

awsAccessKey.description: An accessKey for using S3-storage in the graylog-archiving-plugin

awsSecretKey.description: A secretKey for using S3-storage in the graylog-archiving-plugin

contentDeployPolicy.description: The Graylog content deploy policy (streams, inputs, grok patterns, and so on). The possible values are: - only-create - Only the creation of missing content, without updating the existing content. - force-update - Forcibly updating out-of-the-box content. If the content does not exist, it is created. - skip - Skip all the content create/update tasks.

createIngress.description: Enables / disables creation of graylog ingress .

dockerImage.description: A docker image to use for graylog deployment.

elasticsearchMaxTotalConnections.description: Optional. Maximum number of total connections to Elasticsearch.

elasticsearchMaxTotalConnectionsPerRoute.description: Maximum number of total connections per Elasticsearch route (normally this means per elasticsearch server).

graylogResources.description: The resources describe to compute resource requests and limits for Graylog Pods.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

indexReplicas.description: The number of Elasticsearch replicas used per index.

indexShards.description: The number of Elasticsearch shards used per index.

ingressClassName.description: The name of an IngressClass (ingress controller) cluster resource.

initContainerDockerImage.description: A docker image to initialize plugins for Graylog

initResources.description: The resources describe to compute resource requests and limits for init Pods.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

initSetupImage.description: A docker image for init container for Graylog

inputbufferRingSize.description: Optional. Size of internal ring buffers. Raise this if raising outputbuffer_processors does not help anymore. For optimum performance your LogMessage objects in the ring buffer should fit in your CPU L3 cache. Must be a power of 2. (512, 1024, 2048, ...)

maxNumberOfIndices.description: Set max number of indices in the default index-set

maxSize.description: Set max size for the default index

mongoResources.description: The resources describe to compute resource requests and limits for MongoDB Pods.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

mongoUpgrade.description: Activates automatic step-by-step upgrade of the MongoDB database.

mongodbImage.description: A docker image of MongoDB to use for graylog deployment.

nodeSelectorKey.description: Defines nodes the pod is scheduled on. Used with nodeSelectorValue.

nodeSelectorValue.description: Defines nodes the pod is scheduled on. Used with nodeSelectorKey.

openSearch.description: Configuration for TLS enabling and credentials for OpenSearch.

credentials.description: Kubernetes Secret references for OpenSearch basic auth credentials.

password.description: Reference to the Secret key with OpenSearch password.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the password (e.g. 'password').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the password.

username.description: Reference to the Secret key with OpenSearch username.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the username (e.g. 'user').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the username.

tlsConfig.description: TLS configuration for connecting to OpenSearch.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the CA certificate (e.g. 'cert-ca.pem').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the CA certificate.

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the client certificate (e.g. 'cert.crt').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the client certificate.

insecureSkipVerify.description: Skip server certificate verification when set to true (not recommended for production).

key.description: Key in the Secret data that stores the client private key (e.g. 'cert.key').

name.description: Name of the Secret containing the client private key.

outputBatchSize.description: Optional. Batch size for the Elasticsearch output. This is the maximum (!) number of messages the Elasticsearch output module will get at once and write to Elasticsearch in a batch call. If the configured batch size has not been reached within output_flush_interval seconds, everything that is available will be flushed at once. Remember that every outputbuffer processor manages its own batch and performs its own batch write calls. ("outputbuffer_processors" variable)

outputbufferProcessorThreadsMaxPoolSize.description: Optional. The maximum number of threads to allow in the pool

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName assigned to the Pods to prevent them from evicting.

replicas.description: A number of replicas for Graylog.

ringSize.description: Optional. Size of internal ring buffers. Raise this if raising outputbuffer_processors does not help anymore.

s3Archive.description: A flag for using S3-storage in the graylog-archiving-plugin

securityResources.description: Allows creating security resources such as PodSecurityPolicy, SecurityContextConstraints

install.description: Allow creating security resources as PodSecurityPolicy, SecurityContextConstraints

name.description: Allow creating security resources as PodSecurityPolicy, SecurityContextConstraints

startupTimeout.description: The amount of time the operator waits for a Graylog pod to start, in minutes

maxNumberOfIndices.description: Set max number of indices in the index-set

maxSize.description: Set max size for the index in case of "sizeBased" rotations strategy

name.description: The title of a stream in Graylog.

rotationPeriod.description: Sets rotation period to index set of the stream if rotationStrategy is "sizeBased".

rotationStrategy.description: Sets rotation strategy to index set of the stream.

tls.description: Configuration for TLS enabling for Graylog.

http.description: Configuration for TLS for HTTP interface.

cacerts.description: Contains a name of Secret with CA certificates for a custom CA store.

cert.description: The X.509 certificate chain file in PEM format to use for securing the HTTP interface.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with the certificate.

enabled.description: Allows enabling TLS for HTTP interface.

duration.description: Specifies the validity period of the requested certificate.

enabled.description: Enables certificate generation be cert-manager

renewBefore.description: Defines how long before the certificate’s expiry cert-manager should attempt to renew it.

secretName.description: A name for secret which will store certificates

key.description: The PKCS#8 private key file in PEM format to use for securing the HTTP interface.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the private key.

secretName.description: Name of the Secret with the private key.

keyFilePassword.description: The password to unlock the private key used for securing the HTTP interface.

cert.description: The X.509 certificate chain file in PEM format to use for securing Graylog input. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with the certificate.

enabled.description: Allows enabling TLS for out-of-box GELF input managed by the operator.

duration.description: Specifies the validity period of the requested certificate.

enabled.description: Enabled certificate generation be cert-manager

renewBefore.description: Defines how long before the certificate’s expiry cert-manager should attempt to renew it.

secretName.description: A name for secret which will store certificates

key.description: The PKCS#8 private key file in PEM format to use for securing Graylog input. If present, both secretKey and secretName fields are required.

secretKey.description: Key (filename) in the Secret with the private key for the certificate.

secretName.description: Name of Secret with the private key for the certificate.

keyFilePassword.description: The password to unlock the private key used for securing Graylog input.

externalGraylogServer.description: Flag for specify kind of Graylog (internal in cloud or external on VM).

install.description: Allow disabling create Integration Tests Pod during deploy

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName assigned to the Pods to prevent them from evicting.

resources.description: The resources describe to compute resource requests and limits for Integration Tests Pod.

cpu.description: Maximum amount of CPU resource that can be used by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

cpu.description: Initial and guaranteed amount of CPU resource that will be reserved by a container's process during 1 scheduling period (100ms). Can be integer cores or millicores.

memory.description: Initial amount of memory that will be allocated to a container's process. Can be expressed as a plain integer or as a fixed-point number using one of these quantity suffixes: E, P, T, G, M, k. Also the power-of-two equivalents are available: Ei, Pi, Ti, Gi, Mi, Ki.

name.description: Service name for Integration Tests.

sshKey.description: The parameter specifies the ssh key for login to VM.

customResourcePath.description: Custom resource path for status writing

enabled.description: Enable Store tests status to `LoggingService` custom resource.

isShortStatusMessage.description: The size of integration test status message

onlyIntegrationTests.description: Deploy only integration tests without any component (component was installed before).

tags.description: Tags for Integration Tests. Example: smoke. Possible values: smoke, graylog

timeoutBeforeStart.description: The parameter specifies the delay before starting integration tests.

key.description: Key storing the password for VictoriaLogs.

secretName.description: The parameter specifies the secret name and key storing the password for VictoriaLogs.

key.description: Key storing the token for VictoriaLogs.

secretName.description: The parameter specifies the secret name and key storing the token for VictoriaLogs.

key.description: Key storing the user for VictoriaLogs.

secretName.description: Secret name storing the user for VictoriaLogs.

url.description: The parameter specifies the url for VictoriaLogs.

vmUser.description: The parameter specifies the user for ssh login to VM. Example: ubuntu

ipv6.description: Set to 'true' to deploy to IPv6 environment.

labels.description: Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects. May match selectors of replication controllers and services. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/labels

logLevel.description: The log level of operator logs.

name.description: A name for logging Custom Resource

osKind.description: Operating system kind on cloud nodes: centos/rhel/oracle/ubuntu

install.description: Indicates if qubership-logging-operator has pprof enabled.

title: Logging Service Configuration Schema
